uses: Anogra engelmannii has been traditionally used by Native Americans for its medicinal properties. intro: Anogra engelmannii, also known as Engelmann's anoga or Engelmann's yucca, is a species of flowering plant in the family Asparagaceae. This plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, including wound healing, inflammation, and infections, due to its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Anogra engelmannii include saponins, flavonoids, and triterpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include a topical salve for wound care and an infusion for internal use to support digestive health and immune function.
